title: Rooftop Garden Sensor Grid: Missing-Reading Reconstruction
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; the withheld true readings and hidden data stay in the evaluator process
strategies: per-channel MEAN imputation (fill each hole with its column's observed mean) -- reproduces the evaluator baseline exactly -> ~0.1 on every instance

single-pass linear imputation: mean-fill, then one ridge regression per channel on the (frozen) mean-filled others -- exploits cross-channel correlation but never refines the predictors, so it recovers only part of the low-rank signal

iterative chained-equation (MICE-style) ridge imputation: re-impute every channel from the others over repeated sweeps so estimates co-adapt to the low-rank sensor field -- strong on reconstructable gardens, degrades gracefully to mean-level info on near-full-rank noisy gardens -> high geometric mean across regimes

other viable designs: low-rank SVD / soft-impute matrix completion, kNN-row imputation in the observed subspace, probabilistic PCA / EM, iterative random-forest imputation reconciled to the missing set
